title: pterostylis extranea
intro: Pterostylis extranea, also known as the greenhood orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat coughs, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The plant has been used to relieve symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, making it a valuable resource for promoting overall health and well-being. Pterostylis extranea can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
